persons.arguments_against: Possible objections: (1) one of the other 4 Lugard officials in the corpus is the Hong Kong governor; (2) the staff list shows 'C.M.G.' while the biography has K.C.M.G., hinting at a different man; (3) position_sim FAILed. All fail. The biography states 'gov. of Hong Kong, 1st May, 1907' with the next posting in March 1912 — bracketing the stint's 1911-1912 listing of the colony's sole Governor; the stint name 'Frederick John Dealty' matches the bio's distinctive triple forename 'Frederick John Dealtry' to within one OCR letter, and 'Sir F. J. D. Lugard' matches all three initials; C.B. and D.S.O. are shared, and the 'C.M.G.' is best read as a truncated/garbled K.C.M.G. (1901) — consistent with the 'Sir' styling, since a mere C.M.G. would not be 'Sir'. The position_sim FAIL ('gov.' vs 'Governor', score 55 vs bar 60) is a pure abbreviation artifact. No other Lugard could be Hong Kong's knighted governor with these initials and honours in 1911.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second F. J. D. Lugard serving in Hong Kong in 1911, or a record that the biography subject left the governorship before 1911 and a namesake succeeded him.

notes: Stint window (1911-1912) sits entirely inside the bio's Hong Kong governorship (May 1907 to Mar 1912 transfer to Nigeria). The staff-list 'C.M.G.' vs bio 'K.C.M.G. (1901)' is a likely print/OCR truncation, corroborated by the 'Sir' prefix; C.B. and D.S.O. match exactly.
